label: Narrow-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, SIDE-SPINE STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — three bedrooms above a ~5 m-wide floor. Ground floor: great room across the front (entry, GF circulation), kitchen as a rear-right COLUMN touching the great room's rear edge (>= 2.1 m dining-counter seam) with the T&B tucked beside it, stair as the left-wall spine. Upstairs: master full-width at the front (street windows), then the narrow signature — stairwell + landing hall as a left/mid circulation spine serving br2 mid-right, br3 at the rear, and the second T&B. Canonical 9x13 lot (5x9 buildable per floor, 90 m² gross — in the 3BR/2-bath 80-120 band).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightline
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: second bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-core
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner — lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight (on this deep shell the solver picks the vertical front-to-rear run). common_bath caps stop post-snap gap-filling from stretching a bath into a corridor. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0: the 2F spine hall hosts five doors (three bedrooms, bath, stair arrival) so it earns more area than the 2BR siblings' 4.5, but stays bounded so spare m² goes to bedrooms.

notes: 2-STOREY narrow catalog topology #3 — the narrow take on the 2s 3BR
(authored 2026-07-19; sibling of 2s_3br_wd_rear_stair_baths_ds_gr,
same room program, re-partied for a deep ~5 m-wide shell).
Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF
common T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the spine hall.
All three bedrooms upstairs.
Why the parti differs from the wide sibling: on a wide floor the hall
is a compact mid-band CORE; on a deep narrow floor the circulation
stretches into a left/mid SPINE — stairwell against the left wall
(vertical run, like the 2s narrow 2BR pair) with the landing hall
running beside it, master full-width at the front, br2 against the
right wall mid-plan, br3 at the rear. Every bedroom keeps an exterior
wall (front / right / rear) for windows.
GF kitchen-as-column trick: a deep hall-less GF cannot put a wet band
between great room and kitchen (the kitchen would become unreachable
except through the bath). Instead the kitchen is a rear-right COLUMN
whose head touches the great room's rear edge — preserving the >= 2.1 m
open-plan counter seam AND the rear-wall pin/service door — with the
T&B beside the column's head, doors into the great room.
Canonical layout (9 x 13 lot, 2 m setbacks, 5 x 9 buildable/floor):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear) ]
Rear:  [stair | T&B | kitchen column -> rear service door    ]
2F:  Front: [master (full width)                                  ]
Mid:   [stairwell | hall spine | br2                         ]
Rear:  [T&B       | hall spine | br3                         ]
Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings unless noted, deterministic):
- 9x13 canonical: 90 m² gross; master 15.0 / br2 9.0 / br3 9.0 /
bath2 3.3; GF 96.7% filled (the kitchen claims the rear-left rect
as an L-pantry via the dead-strip decomposition added for this
topology; a ~1.5 m² behind-stair sliver remains — its only
neighbors are stair and bath, both excluded claimants).
- 9x12 (80 gross — band floor): 100% both floors, but bath2 1.3 m²
and br3 6.2 m² sit at hard minimums — the economic edge.
- 8x14 (80 gross, 4 m-wide TRUE-narrow): solves, br2/br3 at 6.0.
- 9x14 (100) and 10x13 (108) also solve; 10x13 carries the
size-dependent kitchen-window soft warning (10%-of-floor rule
outgrows the rear wall — same class as the other 2s topologies).
Structure/override choices: minimal — single-member left_anchored
[stair]; stacks [great,kitchen] [great,bath1] [great,stair] +
[master,hall2] only (the wide sibling's [br2,hall2] stack is dropped:
br2 sits BESIDE the spine, not in front of it). zone_balance_rooms
counts the whole upper floor minus stairwell as private. No zone_split
(the spine geometry emerges from adjacencies alone). rear-anchoring
bath1 was tried and is provably infeasible — bath1 must TOUCH the
great room for its door, so it can never sit on the rear wall.
Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths, `gr` = open great-room style,
no `_hall` token (GROUND floor is hall-less; the 2F landing hall is
mandatory in every 2s topology and is not tokened).
